tossups.question: A disciple of this thinker discussed one of his ideas in terms of a “folded garment” with 231 “gates.” A process discussed by this thinker begins with the formation of the Father and the Small Face among other “faces” in the world of emanation. This thinker imagined a cosmic catastrophe in which light emanated from the Primordial Man and then shattered the vessels that were meant to receive it, causing sparks to be trapped in “husks” called (*) qlippoth (“kleep-OTE”). This thinker lived in Safed and taught Chaim Vital, who collected his teachings like tikkun olam in the book Etz Chaim. According to this thinker, creation began when Ein Sof, the Infinite, performed an act of “withdrawal” or “contraction” called tzimtzum. For 10 points, name this 16th-century rabbi known as “the lion” who pioneered a namesake branch of Kabbalah.
tossups.question_sanitized: A disciple of this thinker discussed one of his ideas in terms of a "folded garment" with 231 "gates." A process discussed by this thinker begins with the formation of the Father and the Small Face among other "faces" in the world of emanation. This thinker imagined a cosmic catastrophe in which light emanated from the Primordial Man and then shattered the vessels that were meant to receive it, causing sparks to be trapped in "husks" called (*) qlippoth ("kleep-OTE"). This thinker lived in Safed and taught Chaim Vital, who collected his teachings like tikkun olam in the book Etz Chaim. According to this thinker, creation began when Ein Sof, the Infinite, performed an act of "withdrawal" or "contraction" called tzimtzum. For 10 points, name this 16th-century rabbi known as "the lion" who pioneered a namesake branch of Kabbalah.
tossups.answer: Isaac Luria [or Yitzchak Luria or the Arizal or Ha’Ari or Ha’Ari Hakodosh] (The disciple in the lead-in is Israel Sarug.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Isaac Luria [or Yitzchak Luria or the Arizal or Ha'Ari or Ha'Ari Hakodosh] (The disciple in the lead-in is Israel Sarug.)

tossups.question: In a novel by this author, a “yellowish individual” is crushed by the stone he drops from a crane in a plot to kill the protagonist at the dedication of a school building. Dr. Ferdinand Blumentritt is exclusively famous for his letters to this author, whose idea of the “devil of comparisons” is invoked in several books by Benedict Anderson. In a novel by this author, a jeweler disguised with blue goggles plans to use a pomegranate-shaped lamp to smuggle dynamite into a wedding feast. A dedication by this author vows to use the ancient method of placing the sick on temple steps to treat his country’s “social cancer.” The friars Salví and Dámaso scheme against the beloved of María Clara in a novel this author followed up with El Filibusterismo (“filibuster-EEZ-mo”). For 10 points, name this author who wrote about Juan Ibarra in Noli me Tangere (“TAHN-jay-ray”), a national hero of the Philippines.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a novel by this author, a "yellowish individual" is crushed by the stone he drops from a crane in a plot to kill the protagonist at the dedication of a school building. Dr. Ferdinand Blumentritt is exclusively famous for his letters to this author, whose idea of the "devil of comparisons" is invoked in several books by Benedict Anderson. In a novel by this author, a jeweler disguised with blue goggles plans to use a pomegranate-shaped lamp to smuggle dynamite into a wedding feast. A dedication by this author vows to use the ancient method of placing the sick on temple steps to treat his country's "social cancer." The friars Salvi and Damaso scheme against the beloved of Maria Clara in a novel this author followed up with El Filibusterismo ("filibuster-EEZ-mo"). For 10 points, name this author who wrote about Juan Ibarra in Noli me Tangere ("TAHN-jay-ray"), a national hero of the Philippines.
tossups.answer: José Rizal [or José Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda; prompt on Realonda]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Jose Rizal [or Jose Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda; prompt on Realonda]

packet.name: W. Emergency 1 - Chicago D, Columbia B, Illinois A, Maryland A_

tossups.question: Charles Derbyshire did a popular translation of this author’s most famous poem, which is inscribed on a bronze monu-ment designed by Richard Kissling titled Motto Stella. In that poem’s frst stanza the speaker proclaims, “Gladly now I go to give thee this faded life’s best, / And were it brighter, fresher, or more blest, / Still would I give it thee, nor count the cost” before concluding, “Beloved creatures all, farewell! In death there is rest!” This author wrote a novel whose protagonist leaves a nitroglycerin-packed lamp set to explode afer twenty minutes as a wedding present afer returning to his home country disguised as the jeweler Simoun. This author wrote “Mi Ultimo Adios” the day before his execution and penned a novel in which Captain Tiago raises Maria Clara and the evil friar Padre Damaso persecutes Crisostomo Ibarra. El Filibusterismo is the sequel to his novel titled afer the Latin words Jesus spoke to Mary Magdalene. For 10 points, name this author who wrote Noli Me Tangere and is the national hero of the Philippines.
tossups.question_sanitized: Charles Derbyshire did a popular translation of this author's most famous poem, which is inscribed on a bronze monu-ment designed by Richard Kissling titled Motto Stella. In death there is rest!" This author wrote a novel whose protagonist leaves a nitroglycerin-packed lamp set to explode afer twenty minutes as a wedding present afer returning to his home country disguised as the jeweler Simoun. This author wrote "Mi Ultimo Adios" the day before his execution and penned a novel in which Captain Tiago raises Maria Clara and the evil friar Padre Damaso persecutes Crisostomo Ibarra. El Filibusterismo is the sequel to his novel titled afer the Latin words Jesus spoke to Mary Magdalene. For 10 points, name this author who wrote Noli Me Tangere and is the national hero of the Philippines.

bonuses.leadin: This author was credited with popularizing a folktale in which a cunning turtle feeds a monkey’s corpse to its friends before tricking them all into drowning. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author who wrote El filibusterismo as a sequel to his novel in which Maria Clara chooses to become a nun after the death of the mestizo Crisóstomo Ibarra.

Nick Joaquin, a Filipino author who wrote about Rizal, used a pen name inspired by this character. An essay by Miguel de Unamuno claims that Rizal is an Oriental version of this gentleman of La Mancha created by Cervantes.
Rizal wrote the poem “Mi último adiós,” or “My Last Farewell,” before dying in this manner. One Hundred Years of Solitude opens with Colonel Aureliano Buendía facing death in this manner.
answers: José Rizal [or José Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda] (The folktale is “The Monkey and the Turtle” and the novel is Noli Me Tángere.)
Don Quixote (The Unamuno essay is “Rizal: The Tagalog Hamlet.” Joaquin’s pen name was “Quijano de Manila.”)

execution by firing squad [accept being shot or killed with guns or similar descriptions] (Gabriel García Márquez wrote One Hundred Years of Solitude.)

The Zohar is an important text in this Jewish mystical tradition. This tradition is often concerned with the relationships between the ten sephirot (“suh-fee-ROTE”), which are emanations from Ein Sof (“ane SOHF”), the infinite god.

Another influential teacher in Kabbalah was this man, whose ideas were collected by Chaim Vital (“KHAH-yeem vee-TAHL”) in the Etz Chayim (“etts KHAH-yeem”). This man taught that creation arose when God contracted himself during tzimtzum (“tseem-TSOOM”).
answers: Simeon bar Yochai (“yoh-HY”) [or Simeon ben Yohai; or Rashbi, but be careful not to accept “Rashi”]

bonuses.leadin: At the end of this novel, readers learn that Father Salvi regularly used Captain Tiago’s daughter, a nun, for sex. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Frey Damaso convinces Tiago to disallow Maria Clara from marrying the protagonist after that protagonist gets excommunicated from the Catholic Church.
This Filipino author and revolutionary wrote Noli Me Tangere and its sequel, El Filibusterismo.

Ambeth, a scholar with this last name, tried to reconstruct the final novel of Jose Rizal. Other authors with this surname include the only female who helped compile The Book of Fantasy, and a sibling of that author who published the literary magazine Sur.
answers: Ocampo [Those writers are Ambeth, Silvia, and Victoria Ocampo.]
answers_sanitized: Noli Me Tangere [or Touch Me Not]
Jose [Protacio] Rizal [Mercado y Alonso Realonda]
Ocampo [Those writers are Ambeth, Silvia, and Victoria Ocampo.]

Ambeth, a scholar with this last name, tried to reconstruct the final novel of Jose Rizal. Other authors with this surname include the only female who helped compile The Book of Fantasy, and a sibling of that author who published the literary magazine Sur.

bonuses.leadin: This writer was conversant in 22 languages, and his namesake law mandates that every school in his native country must offer courses about him. For 10 points each:
parts: This man wrote about a San Diego landlord, Capitán Tiago, and the son of a businessman, Ibarra, in Noli Me Tangere, as well as the sequel El Filibusterismo.
Rizal wrote this poem the night before his execution. This poem was later read to the U. S. Congress by Henry Cooper.

Rizal was executed by the Spanish on December 30, 1896, because he desired greater autonomy for this country. Other authors from this country include Francisco Balagtas, who wrote Florante at Laura in Tagalog.
answers: José Protacio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda
Mi último adiós [accept My Final Farewell and other close equivalents]

Rizal was executed by the Spanish on December 30, 1896, because he desired greater autonomy for this country. Other authors from this country include Francisco Balagtas, who wrote Florante at Laura in Tagalog.

bonuses.leadin: This man wrote about Spanish abuses in his country in novels like The Social Cancer and The Reign of Greed. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this nationalist who wrote a “Last Farewell” before being executed at Fort Santiago in 1896. His country would eventually be ruled by native-born leaders like Ferdinand Marcos.
Rizal sought reforms, although not independence, for this modern day nation. Its capital, located on the island of Luzon, was largely destroyed during World War II.
The namesake Communist group in this post-war rebellion in the Philippines fought the Japanese after the fall of Bataan. President Manuel Roxas adopted a “mailed fist” policy against this group.
answers: José Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda
answers_sanitized: Jose Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda
Republic of the Philippines [or Pilipinas]
Hukbalahap Rebellion

parts_sanitized: Identify this nationalist who wrote a "Last Farewell" before being executed at Fort Santiago in 1896. His country would eventually be ruled by native-born leaders like Ferdinand Marcos.
Rizal sought reforms, although not independence, for this modern day nation. Its capital, located on the island of Luzon, was largely destroyed during World War II.
The namesake Communist group in this post-war rebellion in the Philippines fought the Japanese after the fall of Bataan. President Manuel Roxas adopted a "mailed fist" policy against this group.

bonuses.leadin: This poem's speaker hopes that a bird landing on his marker will sing “a song of peace,” and announces that he goes where there are “no hangmen / Where faith does not kill, where the one who reigns is God.” For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this poem that ends “Farewell to all I love. To die is to rest,” whose title arose organically after it was recovered untitled by the author's close friends and family.

This national hero and major literary figure of the Philippines wrote “Mi Ultimo Adios” shortly before his execution for supporting the Katipunan. He is best known for his novel Noli me Tangere and its sequel, El Filibusterismo.

Another major Filipino author, Carlos Bulosan, is best known for describing his immigration to the U.S. in America is in the Heart, a work often compared to this John Steinbeck novel about the Joad family's flight from the Dust Bowl.
answers: “Mi Ultimo Adios” [or “My Last Farewell;” accept any variations involving the phrase “Last Farewell” as translations differ]
answers_sanitized: "Mi Ultimo Adios" [or "My Last Farewell;" accept any variations involving the phrase "Last Farewell" as translations differ]
Jose Rizal
The Grapes of Wrath
